questions.q: What is the difference between Gross National Product (GNP) and Gross Domestic Product (GDP)?
opts: (A) GNP includes production of foreigners within the country and GDP includes production of residents outside the country.
(B) GNP includes production of residents within the country and GDP excludes production of residents outside the country.
(C) GNP excludes production of foreigners within the country and GDP includes production of residents outside the country.
(D) GNP and GDP are same and there is no difference between them.
questions.explanation: GNP is the total value of goods and services produced by the citizens of a country within or outside the country. GDP is the total value of goods and services produced within the country.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Gross National Product (GNP)?
opts: (A) GNP = C + I + G + (X - M)
(B) GNP = C + I + G + X - M
(C) GNP = C + I + G + (X - M) + (R - T)
(D) GNP = C + I + G + X
questions.explanation: The formula for GNP is the sum of consumption, investment, government expenditure and net exports (exports minus imports). Net factor income from abroad is also added to GNP.

questions.q: What is the difference between Net National Product (NNP) and Net Domestic Product (NDP)?
opts: (A) NNP includes depreciation whereas NDP excludes depreciation.
(B) NNP excludes depreciation whereas NDP includes depreciation.
(C) NNP and NDP are same and there is no difference between them.
(D) NNP excludes capital consumption and NDP includes capital consumption.
questions.explanation: NNP is the total value of goods and services produced within the country, minus depreciation. NDP is the total value of goods and services produced within the country, minus capital consumption.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Net National Product (NNP)?
opts: (A) NNP = GDP + Depreciation
(B) NNP = GDP - Depreciation
(C) NNP = GDP + Depreciation + Net Factor Income from Abroad
(D) NNP = GDP - Depreciation - Net Factor Income from Abroad
questions.explanation: The formula for NNP is GDP minus depreciation.

questions.q: What is the concept of Personal Income and Disposable Income?
opts: (A) Personal Income is the income earned by individuals from various sources and Disposable Income is the income left after paying taxes.
(B) Personal Income is the income left after paying taxes and Disposable Income is the income earned by individuals from various sources.
(C) Personal Income is the income left after paying taxes and Disposable Income is same as Personal Income.
(D) Personal Income and Disposable Income are same and there is no difference between them.
questions.explanation: Personal Income is the income earned by individuals from various sources, while Disposable Income is the income left after paying taxes.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Personal Income (PI)?
opts: (A) PI = W + P + T
(B) PI = W + P - T
(C) PI = W + T - P
(D) PI = W - P - T
questions.explanation: The formula for Personal Income (PI) is the sum of wages, profits and transfers.

questions.q: What is the concept of Per Capita Income?
opts: (A) Per Capita Income is the total income of a country divided by the population.
(B) Per Capita Income is the average income of each person in a country.
(C) Per Capita Income is the total income of a country divided by the number of employed people.
(D) Per Capita Income is the total income of a country divided by the number of businesses.
questions.explanation: Per Capita Income is the total income of a country divided by the population.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Gross Domestic Product (GDP)?
opts: (A) GDP = C + I + G + (X - M)
(B) GDP = C + I + G - (X - M)
(C) GDP = C + I + (X - M) - G
(D) GDP = C + G - (X - M) + I
questions.explanation: The formula for GDP is the sum of consumption, investment, government expenditure and net exports (exports minus imports).

questions.q: What is the difference between Gross National Expenditure (GNE) and Gross National Product (GNP)?
opts: (A) GNE includes investment and GNP excludes investment.
(B) GNE excludes investment and GNP includes investment.
(C) GNE and GNP are same and there is no difference between them.
(D) GNE includes net factor income from abroad and GNP excludes net factor income from abroad.
questions.explanation: GNE is the total expenditure of the country on goods and services, while GNP is the total value of goods and services produced by the citizens of a country within or outside the country.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Net Domestic Product (NDP)?
opts: (A) NDP = GDP - (Wages + Depreciation)
(B) NDP = GDP - Depreciation
(C) NDP = GDP - (Wages + Depreciation + Interest)
(D) NDP = GDP - Interest
questions.explanation: The formula for NDP is GDP minus depreciation.

questions.q: What is the concept of Factor Income?
opts: (A) Factor Income is the income earned by factors of production such as labor and capital.
(B) Factor Income is the income earned by individuals from various sources.
(C) Factor Income is the income left after paying taxes.
(D) Factor Income is the income earned by businesses from sales.
questions.explanation: Factor Income is the income earned by factors of production such as labor and capital.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Net National Disposable Income (NNDI)?
opts: (A) NNDI = NNP + Net Factor Income from Abroad - Personal Income Tax
(B) NNDI = NNP + Personal Income Tax - Net Factor Income from Abroad
(C) NNDI = NNP - Net Factor Income from Abroad + Personal Income Tax
(D) NNDI = NNP - Personal Income Tax + Net Factor Income from Abroad
questions.explanation: The formula for NNDI is NNP plus net factor income from abroad minus personal income tax.

questions.q: What is the concept of Per Capita Income in terms of Net National Product (NNP)?
opts: (A) Per Capita Income in terms of NNP is the total NNP divided by the population.
(B) Per Capita Income in terms of NNP is the average income of each person in a country.
(C) Per Capita Income in terms of NNP is the total NNP divided by the number of employed people.
(D) Per Capita Income in terms of NNP is the total NNP divided by the number of businesses.
questions.explanation: Per Capita Income in terms of NNP is the total NNP divided by the population.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Per Capita Income?
opts: (A) Per Capita Income = Total Income / Population
(B) Per Capita Income = Average Income / Number of Employed People
(C) Per Capita Income = Total Income / Number of Businesses
(D) Per Capita Income = Average Income / Number of Businesses
questions.explanation: The formula for Per Capita Income is the total income divided by the population.

questions.q: What is the concept of Gross Capital Formation (GCF)?
opts: (A) GCF is the total value of goods and services produced by a country.
(B) GCF is the total value of goods and services consumed by a country.
(C) GCF is the total value of goods and services invested in a country.
(D) GCF is the total value of goods and services exported by a country.
questions.explanation: GCF is the total value of goods and services invested in a country.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Gross Capital Formation (GCF)?
opts: (A) GCF = I + D
(B) GCF = I - D
(C) GCF = I + (X - M)
(D) GCF = I - (X - M)
questions.explanation: The formula for GCF is the sum of investment and depreciation.

questions.q: What is the concept of Saving?
opts: (A) Saving is the income earned by individuals from various sources.
(B) Saving is the income left after paying taxes.
(C) Saving is the income earned by businesses from sales.
(D) Saving is the income left after paying taxes and consumption.
questions.explanation: Saving is the income left after paying taxes and consumption.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Saving?
opts: (A) Saving = Disposable Income - Consumption
(B) Saving = Disposable Income + Consumption
(C) Saving = Disposable Income - (Consumption + Taxes)
(D) Saving = Disposable Income + (Consumption + Taxes)
questions.explanation: The formula for Saving is Disposable Income minus Consumption.

questions.q: What is the concept of Investment?
opts: (A) Investment is the income earned by individuals from various sources.
(B) Investment is the income left after paying taxes.
(C) Investment is the income earned by businesses from sales.
(D) Investment is the sum of savings and consumption.
questions.explanation: Investment is the income earned by businesses from sales.

questions.q: What is the formula for calculating Investment?
opts: (A) Investment = Saving + Consumption
(B) Investment = Saving - Consumption
(C) Investment = Saving + (Consumption + Taxes)
(D) Investment = Saving - (Consumption + Taxes)
questions.explanation: The formula for Investment is Saving minus Consumption.
